findings.message: Hook matcher Edit|Write|Bash fires on every single tool invocation. Internal guards exist in post-tool.md but hook setup overhead runs unconditionally on all file edits and shell commands.
When auto-capture is enabled, all significant Bash command details are written to .nemp-pro/activity.log without sanitizing for sensitive argument patterns like --token, --key, --password, --secret
marketplace.json plugins[0].version is 0.1.0 but plugin.json declares version 0.3.0 — these must match for correct marketplace indexing
marketplace.json version 0.1.0 does not match plugin.json version 0.3.0 — two minor versions behind
CLAUDE.md structure value says commands/ (20 .md files) but the repo contains 24 command .md files — count became stale as new commands were added after last /nemp:init run
Root SKILL.md has valid frontmatter (name + description) but an empty body — no skill reference content, usage guidance, or memory schema documentation
Step 4 confirmation message lists /nemp:export --codex / --cursor / --windsurf / --all instead of the correct /nemp-pro:export --* syntax
activate.md confirmation references /nemp:export --codex/--cursor/--windsurf/--all instead of /nemp-pro:export --*, creating inconsistency with the actual Pro command namespace
Command file declares no allowed-tools; uses Write for license.json
Command file declares no allowed-tools; uses Bash, Read, AskUserQuestion
Command file declares no allowed-tools; uses Bash, Read, Write
Command file declares no allowed-tools; uses Bash, Read, Write
Command file declares no allowed-tools; uses Bash, Read, Write
Command file declares no allowed-tools; uses Bash, Read, Write
7 vague quantifier instances: intelligent, smart (x4 in section titles), beautiful, relevant
Stub command declares no allowed-tools in frontmatter
Stub command declares no allowed-tools in frontmatter
Pro section lists /nemp:export --codex/--cursor/--windsurf/--all but the correct Pro commands are /nemp-pro:export --codex etc.
export.md Pro section uses /nemp:export namespace for Pro features that actually live under /nemp-pro:export, contradicting nemp-pro-export.md which correctly defines the Pro namespace
Command file declares no allowed-tools; uses Bash, Read, Write
3 vague quantifier instances: clean, professional, relevant

Command file declares no allowed-tools; uses Bash, Read, Write, AskUserQuestion
forget.md contains two separate '### 7.' headings in sequence — steps run 1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8,7 with the second 7 handling multiple-match logic out of sequence

Entire skill body uses backslash-escaped markdown syntax (\# for headings, \*\* for bold, \_ for italic) which renders as literal backslashes in all standard parsers instead of formatting
